feeBasis: The $50 registration fee is the same for every owner type and filing path, and renewal costs another $50. The newspaper advertisement required before filing is paid separately at the paper's own rate.
renewalRule: Renewable between January 1 and December 31 of the expiration year for another 5-year period.
publicationRule: The name must be advertised at least once in a newspaper in the county of the registrant's principal place of business before registering, and the application certifies that this has been done. Fla. Stat. § 865.09(3).
protectionNote: Registration is for public notice only. Fla. Stat. § 865.09(8) states that it does not give rise to a presumption of the registrant's rights to own or use the name and does not affect trademark or entity-name rights previously acquired by others.
searchNote: Sunbiz has a free statewide fictitious name search. Florida is one of the few states where every registration sits in one public index.
notes: A business operating under an unregistered fictitious name cannot maintain a lawsuit in Florida courts until it registers. Every registration expires on December 31 of the fifth year regardless of the month it was filed, so a January registration holds almost a full year longer than a December one.
